name: Oil Industry Suppression
description: The "Oil Industry Suppression" theory posits that advanced, non-fossil fuel energy technologies have been deliberately suppressed by powerful entities, primarily the oil industry, to maintain their control over the global energy market and protect their vast profits. Proponents claim that inventions like free energy devices, cold fusion, and high-efficiency electric engines have been successfully developed but systematically squashed through patents being bought up and shelved, inventors being discredited or silenced, and research funding being redirected. The alleged motive is to prevent the disruption of the existing fossil fuel-based economy and the potential loss of trillions of dollars in revenue. Origins of the theory can be traced back to the late 19th and early 20th centuries with the rise of oil as a dominant energy source and anxieties about monopolies. Mainstream science and economics largely dismiss the theory due to a lack of verifiable evidence, the violation of established scientific principles in many proposed alternative energy technologies, and the inherent difficulties of maintaining a global conspiracy of such scale. Academics argue that genuine breakthroughs in energy technology would be rapidly adopted and commercialized due to the immense economic incentives, even if opposed by established industries, and that the challenges of developing and scaling such technologies are often underestimated by proponents.
subtitle: Suppressed energy technologies and conspiracies

claims.claim: Advanced free energy technologies exist but are suppressed.
claims.evidence: Testimonials from inventors, anecdotal evidence of working prototypes, patents bought up and shelved.
claims.criticism: Lack of independently verifiable evidence, violation of established laws of thermodynamics, many claims based on perpetual motion machines.

claims.claim: The oil industry actively buys and suppresses patents for alternative energy technologies.
claims.evidence: Claims of companies purchasing patents only to shelve them, historical examples of industries stifling competition.
claims.criticism: Patent acquisition is a common business practice; many shelved patents are simply not commercially viable or technically sound. Burden of proof lies on showing intent to suppress rather than develop.

claims.claim: Inventors of free energy devices are harassed, threatened, or discredited.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of inventors facing difficulties, alleged smear campaigns.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence, confirmation bias in interpreting events as deliberate suppression, tendency to romanticize inventor's struggles.

keyFigures.name: Nikola Tesla
keyFigures.role: Considered by some as an early victim of energy suppression (though primarily related to wireless power transmission).

keyFigures.name: Eugene Mallove
keyFigures.role: Vocal proponent of cold fusion and critic of mainstream energy research; died under suspicious circumstances.

keyFigures.name: Brian Dunning
keyFigures.role: Science writer and skeptic who has debunked many free energy claims.

mythologyConnections: Prometheus (stealing fire from the gods - representing access to advanced knowledge)
The idea of forbidden knowledge and its consequences

timeline.event: Rise of the oil industry and emergence of anxieties about monopolies.
Nikola Tesla's work on wireless energy transmission and purported suppression.
Announcement of cold fusion by Pons and Fleischmann, followed by controversy and eventual discrediting.
Continued claims of suppressed energy technologies and the rise of online communities dedicated to the topic.

sources.author: Brian Dunning
sources.work: Skeptoid Podcast: The Free Energy Conspiracy
sources.title: Brian Dunning
